01 — Why Football

A $50bn market
driven by structural scarcity.

Football is one of the largest, most liquid and fastest-growing global sports markets. Crisis-resilient. AI-resistant. Structurally non-cyclical.

Capital moves late in football, driven by hype, urgency and fear of missing out. We operate early, before the market prices the asset.

+40%
Transfer value CAGR Average South American player transfer value grows at approximately 40% year over year.
$50B
Global football market Media rights, sponsorships, ticketing and player trading, a market that grew through multiple recessions and a global pandemic.
6+
Month value realisation cycles Short investment cycles with asymmetric upside. Entry at early-stage pricing, exit at international market pricing.
02 — The Vision

The world's leading multi-club groups, City Football Group, Red Bull, and the new generation of European platforms, all face the same structural gap: access to South American talent at the source. Best Eleven is that missing piece: the supply base, already operating, in the region's three deepest talent markets.

What European Multi-Club Groups Need
  • Early access to South American talent at the source
  • First-look rights before the market prices players
  • Local operating structures they cannot build alone
  • Regulatory-clean supply, no UEFA multi-club conflicts
  • Talent shaped to professional European standards
  • A trusted, institutional counterparty in the region
→
What Best Eleven Delivers
  • Operating clubs in Argentina, Paraguay, Uruguay & USA
  • Proprietary scouting system: +11,000 players monitored
  • The Semillero: a full talent production pipeline
  • Assets from 3rd division to Primera, full ladder coverage
  • 20+ years of relationship capital in football markets
  • Export-ready players for European platforms

European groups have built the destination: flagship clubs and bridge structures. What none of them has built is the source. Best Eleven is the South American supply engine, already operating, with proven exits and institutional relationships. What we are raising capital for is scale acceleration.

Player Production Pipeline
The Semillero Model

Investment in player development across Argentina, Uruguay, and Paraguay gives us early access to the region's best emerging talent. Early recruitment allows us to compete for the next generation of top players and shape them according to Best Eleven's methods, values, and conditioning infrastructure, before the market prices them.

The long-term goal: the greatest assets our clubs produce will not come from scouting. They will come from within: players developed entirely inside the Best Eleven ecosystem, export-ready for European multi-club platforms seeking first access to South American talent.

05 — Colegiales, Argentina

Top 10
Argentine
football
by 2031.

We took over a club in the 3rd division with a squad value under USD 1.5M. In the first year we earned promotion to the 2nd division, the first time in 70 years. With 9 years of management remaining, the next move is the Primera División, and we're building the infrastructure to stay there. The stadium already belongs to Club Atlético Colegiales, BESG's capital improves a shared institutional asset.

Colegiales becomes the Argentine anchor of the group: a development and export machine operating from a modernised stadium in Buenos Aires.

2026

Stadium renovation initiated

Capacity raised toward 10,000. Render design underway. Infrastructure investment unlocks commercial revenue and club valuation.

2027

Promotion playoff to Primera División

Two-season squad budget increase drives competitive performance.

2029

Primera División: sustained

Own training facility operational. Player pipeline from Rubio Ñu and Lito feeds the squad. First Copa Sudamericana classification.

2031

Top 1st Division talent export team

Recognised developer and exporter of elite talent. USD 5M+ annual player sales. International sponsorship deals. Candidate for Copa Libertadores.

Copa
Libertadores
by 2027.

07 — Centro Atlético Lito, Uruguay

Primera
División
Uruguay
by 2028.

Acquired in November 2025. The club is 100% owned by BESG, fully financially restructured and operationally independent. Squad value already grew from under USD 100K to over USD 1.6M.

The Uruguayan 3rd Division has gained international prestige: Suárez, Messi, Forlán and Muslera have all invested here. Promotion to the 2nd Division brings +600% increase in TV rights alone.

2026

Promotion to 2nd Division

TV rights jump from near-zero to approximately USD +700K/year (+600%). Financial sustainability reached within the season.

2027

Own training facility & stadium

Land purchased and construction begins in western Montevideo, an underdeveloped talent zone with no established clubs. Own asset on BESG balance sheet.

2028

Primera División. Copa pipeline.

Established in the top flight. Semillero of European-level talent. Copa Sudamericana and Libertadores classification within the strategic plan.

Strategic Advantages
SAD Structure
100% owned. Fully cleaned up. Operationally independent from original civil association. Clean cap table from day one.
TV Rights Leverage
+600% increase upon promotion to 2nd Division: ~USD +700K/year. In Primera División: USD 2M/year, the club becomes fully self-sustaining.
Already self-sustaining in 2nd division.
Underdeveloped Territory
Western Montevideo focus. All major clubs concentrate efforts east of the city. First-mover advantage in talent identification and youth recruitment.
Federation Infrastructure Support
Dedicated budget for facility improvements starting 2027 for clubs remaining in the division. External capital contributes to a club already receiving institutional support.

08 — Miami United FC*, USA

The US
Gateway.

Miami United FC is 100% owned by a BESG partner and currently operates in the 1st Semi-Professional Division. It sits outside BESG equity, but is fully available to the project. The club is the strategic US anchor of the platform: a development and showcase window for South American talent entering the North American market.

The club competes in a market with one of the highest concentrations of football capital in the Americas, alongside Inter Miami CF. This creates a unique structural opportunity: potentially become the preferred development partner and MLS Next Pro affiliate for Inter Miami, and ultimately, position the club as a candidate for a second MLS franchise in Miami.

Now

1st Semi-Pro Division · owned by a BESG partner

Competitive results. Player showcase platform. South American talent enters US football through BESG's controlled environment.

2027

MLS Next Pro affiliate: strategic target

Potential strategic partnership with Inter Miami CF. Miami United is positioned to become a preferred development and reserve pathway, with access to MLS infrastructure, scouting networks and broadcast opportunities.

2030+

Second MLS franchise in Miami

Miami is one of the few US markets capable of supporting two MLS teams. BESG positions Miami United as the candidate, leveraging established fanbase, infrastructure investment and MLS Next Pro track record.

Strategic Value to BESG
→US market entry with 100% ownership from day one
→Showcase window for South American talent to European and MLS scouts
→Multi-club internal rotation: players develop across Colegiales, Rubio, Lito and arrive in Miami market-ready
→An MLS Next Pro affiliate deal would be transformative: broadcast revenue, training infrastructure access, and direct pipeline to one of MLS's highest-profile clubs
→MLS expansion franchise: the highest-value exit path in US football
Revenue Potential
MLS Next Pro (if affiliate)Broadcast + prize revenue
Player transfers to MLSStructural premium
Sponsorships (Miami market)High-value commercial base
MLS franchise valuation$400M–$800M range (market comp)
Facilities · Hialeah, Miami
Hialeah · Miami · Multi-sport facility
Miami United · Football in the US & MLS
* Miami United FC is owned 100% by a BESG partner. It is not part of the BESG equity structure in this raise, but is available to the project and may join formally under its own individual business plan and capital raise.
09 — Brand & Retail

Clubs that win on the pitch,
and become brands off it.

Best Eleven is built to be recognised for what we do on the field and beyond it. Distinctive kits and apparel turn each club into a brand: they fill stadiums, spread the colours through the city, and convert fans into a recurring commercial base. Official stores are live in all three markets.

Colegiales · Buenos Aires
Rubio Ñu · La Arboleda, Asunción
Lito · Montevideo
Identity that travels
Strong kit and apparel design carries the colours beyond the stadium: every shirt sold is a billboard for the club.
Fans into the ground
A real brand fills stands, grows membership and deepens the local base each club competes for.
Recurring revenue · upside
Merchandising and retail compound year over year and are not included in the base-case projections.
Official stores live · Rubio Ñu (La Arboleda, Asunción) · Lito (Montevideo) · Colegiales (Buenos Aires)
10 — Representation Agency

Representation: revenue
beyond our own clubs.

We extend the platform into player and coaching-staff representation, leveraging the same scouting network and 20+ years of relationships. The agency monetises talent we do not own, with no borders on the market we can serve, and turns transactions into recurring commission income.

Contrato de Representación Deportiva · Best Eleven Sports Group

$100K

2029

2031

Scaled agency, recurring commissions
Players & coaching staff
Representation across both athletes and technical staff widens the commissionable base.
No borders
The agency serves talent beyond our own clubs, into European and global markets.
Recurring revenue · upside
Commission income compounds and is not included in the base-case projections.
11 — Financial Projections

Group revenue,
modelled two ways.

We show both a conservative case (sales and prize income haircut ~40%, slower promotions) and the target case built bottom-up from each club. The investment thesis holds in both.

Year Conservative Target
2026$1.5M$3M
2027$3.5M$6M
2028$6M$10M
2031$15M$22M
Revenue Channels
Player transfers · TV rights · Cup prizes (Libertadores ~$3.5M, Sudamericana ~$1.5M per classification) · Sponsorship · Facility monetisation · Player representation · Group-level exit.
Key Assumptions
Conservative case assumes one fewer promotion, ~40% lower transfer realisation, and no Libertadores prize income before 2028. Even so, group revenue compounds to $15M by 2031.
12 — The Deal & Returns

What you put in. What you own.
What you get back.

A $25M raise is structured in three tranches so capital is matched to risk: growth equity where the upside is, hard assets where the security is.

$9M
Growth equity
Squad budgets across Colegiales, Rubio Ñu & Lito. Direct equity in BESG holding.
$14M
Real-asset tranche
Land, stadiums & facilities. Secured by the real estate: appreciating BESG balance-sheet assets.
$2M
Founder alignment
Released only against sporting milestones ($300K per Sudamericana, $1M per Libertadores). Founders paid when the platform performs.

Governance & Ownership
Full legal control of all clubs. Assembly-approved agreements in place. SAD structure in Uruguay. Facilities built by BESG are BESG assets. Investment is equity in the group holding entity, with full upside across all assets and exit paths.
Why the Founder Tranche is Structured This Way
The $2M is not a day-one cash-out. It releases only as the platform hits Copa qualifications, aligning founders with the same outcomes that drive your return.
14 — Risks & Mitigation

We've priced
the downside.

Football is volatile. The platform is built so that no single sporting outcome breaks the investment.

Sporting risk · relegation / no promotion
Mitigation: four clubs across four markets diversify outcomes. Real estate retains value independent of division. Conservative case assumes missed promotions.
Transfer-market & player risk
Mitigation: A dedicated scouting platform of 9 people, active 24/7, reaching the most remote talent hubs across South America. Pipeline, not one-off bets.
FX & macro (Argentina)
Mitigation: transfers and prize income are USD/EUR-denominated. Hard assets hold value in dollar terms; revenue is export-led.
Regulatory & governance
Mitigation: regulatory-clean structure with no UEFA multi-club conflicts. SAD in Uruguay; assembly-approved management agreements elsewhere.
Capital protection: ~$14M of the raise buys hard, appreciating real estate on the group balance sheet, a downside floor that exists regardless of any sporting result.
15 — The Ask

Two ways
to invest.

Same clubs, same operating team, same scouting platform. Choose how much of the capital goes into real estate versus pure sporting performance. Both plans include $2M in flexible player-investment opportunities, deployed over two years.

Option A
$27M
Full Platform
Squad budgets plus real estate: stadiums, training facilities and land, building permanent BESG assets across all three clubs.
View full breakdown →
Option B
$13M
Sporting Performance
Pure sporting capital: squad budgets and founder milestones only. No real estate investment.

Use of Funds

Player acquisition, squad salaries and management agreements. Stadium and facility construction. Club operations and executive team across all markets.

Investment Structure

Equity in Best Eleven Sports Group (BESG). Direct participation in the group holding entity, not individual clubs. Full upside across all assets, all markets, all exit paths.

Governance & Asset Ownership

Full legal and contractual control of all clubs. Assembly-approved agreements in place. SAD structure in Uruguay. Facilities acquired or built by BESG are BESG assets, available to managed clubs under agreement but not club property. Exception: Colegiales stadium, which belongs to the club; BESG investment improves a shared asset.

Revenue Channels

Player transfers · TV rights · Cup prizes · Sponsorships · Facility monetisation · Player representation · Player investment returns · Scouting consulting · Group-level exit

Group Revenue Target
$29M+
Annual group revenue by 2031 across all clubs and verticals
Additional Return · Player Investments
+$4M
Projected proceeds from the $2M player-investment pool, on top of each club's own sales pipeline.
Real Estate Income & Appreciation
$1M+/yr
Facility rental income across owned assets, plus land and stadium appreciation, independent of sporting outcomes.
BESG Projected Value 2035
$150M
6x group revenue ($25M), in line with recent multi-club transaction multiples
16 — Value Creation & Returns

Equity in a platform
built to compound.

Real Asset Appreciation
Three clubs with physical assets: stadiums, training facilities, land, sitting on the BESG balance sheet. Real estate in key football markets with permanent institutional value.
Player Transfer Revenue
USD 10M+ annual player sales from Rubio Ñu alone by 2028. Colegiales adds USD 5M+. Lito builds its pipeline. Compounding, repeatable revenue from the core asset factory.
Cup Prize Income
Copa Libertadores: ~USD 3.5M. Copa Sudamericana: ~USD 1.5M per classification. Additional prize money per round advanced. Rubio Ñu alone targets USD 5M annually from 2028.
Group-Level Exit
BESG as a consolidated entity: multiple clubs, proprietary scouting tech, +11,000 player database, international network, is a premium acquisition target for larger sports groups, sovereign funds and institutional investors.
2026 Capital deployed
Operations scaled
2028 All clubs in top
national divisions
2029 Group revenue
> USD 20M/year
2031 Strategic exit
or reinvestment
17 — The Team

Operators, not
spectators.

Gonzalo Siegrist
Sporting Director & Partner
Head Scout for South America, Liverpool FC. Leads all sporting strategy, talent identification, and the Semillero development pipeline across BESG clubs.
Jorge Ignacio Connolly Uria
President · Investor & Partner
President of Best Eleven Sports Group. Investor and founding partner driving group strategy, capital structure, and institutional relationships.
Sergio Puerta
Head of Transfer Market
Leads BESG's transfer market operations: player valuation, negotiation, and placement across South American and European markets.

This is not a concept.
It is an operating platform,
ready to scale.

Best Eleven Sports Group is seeking a strategic partner who understands institutional football at the highest level. This is an invitation to co-build South America's defining multi-club platform, at the ground floor, before the market prices it.

Elite scouting. Disciplined capital. Controlled execution. Full-stack football operations across four countries, entered below tangible squad value and modelled to a 5–8x return.

Request Full Financials & Data Room
